description: A microbenchmark whose result is unused, so the compiler optimizes the work away and the timing measures nothing.
body: Failure mode: the benchmarked computation's result is never consumed, so the JIT/compiler eliminates it as dead code and the loop times an empty body, reporting implausibly fast results. Detection signals: near-zero or impossibly fast per-iteration times; results that do not scale with input size; assembly/profile shows the work removed. Do not leave benchmark results unconsumed. Safer fallback: sink the result to a volatile/blackhole, vary inputs so work cannot be precomputed, and sanity-check that timings scale with problem size.
